medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient is a ___ y/o female transferred with a probable ACOMM aneurysm for further evaluation and possible intervention. She is s/p suboccipital craniectomy in ___ at ___, which was followed by a long hospital course, including placement of a trach and PEG. She was discharged to a rehabilitation facility, where drainage from her suboccipital incision was noted, leading to readmission. During this admission, she underwent placement of a VP shunt. A probable ACOMM aneurysm was noted, and a direct transfer to Neurosurgery was scheduled.
medical_history.past_medical_history: History of recent IVH, cerebellar hemorrhage s/p suboccipital craniectomy, placement of VPS on the right, Type II DM, obesity, asthma, hypertension.

physical_examination.general_examination: On admission: T: 98.7, BP: 150/74, HR: 78, RR: 20, O2Sats 96% RA. General: Lying in bed, no acute distress. Neuro: Mental status awake, alert, cooperative, oriented to person only by mouthing words.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Right ptosis, lower right facial droop, strength full power throughout biceps, triceps, grip strength, deltoid strength 4+/5 on the right. Cranial nerves were examined; hearing intact to voice. No drainage from incisions but slight erythema noted.
